Liu Yan (Weishuo)
劉琰 (威碩)
Lived AD ?–264

Liu Yan, styledWeishuo, was fromLu state[魯國].
When theFormer Lordwas inYu province[豫州], he [Liu Yan] was madeClerk. Because of his agnate surname, his demeanor, and his affinity for discussion, he was treated especially generously. Thus, he followed in expeditions and often acted as a retainer. When theFormer LordstabilizedYi province[益州],[Liu] Yanwas madeGrand AdministratorofGuling[固陵太守].
After theLatter Lordwas enthroned, he was enfeoffed asMarquis of a Capital Township. His status and rank was always second to that ofLi Yan. He served asMinister of the Guards,Central Military Adviser, andGeneral of the Rear, then was promoted toGeneral of Chariots and Cavalry. However, he did not participate in governance of the empire and he only led over one thousand troops. He followedAssisting Chancellor[Zhuge] Liangin giving advice and comments, nothing more. His vehicles, dresses, drinks, and meals were extravagant. He had tens of servants and maids, all of whom were capable of singing and dancing, and he had them all learn to recite theRhapsody of the Hall of Spiritual Resplendence inLu[魯靈光殿賦].1•••
This rhapsody has over one thousand and five hundred characters, making it extremely hard to memorize. It contains effusive praise for the Hall of Spiritual Resplendence, going into extreme detail about its structure in a very proud manner.↩
In the tenth year of theera [232], as he and theFront Military Adviser,Wei Yan, were not harmonious with each other, he spoke false and absurd words, so[Zhuge] Liangrebuked him.[Liu] Yanwrote a letter of apology to[Zhuge] Liang, stating:
“I am shallow and pretentious in personality, having poor moral conduct and being disposed to alcohol and laziness. Ever since [the time I was with] thedeparted emperor, my numerous, motley opinions almost led to serious damage.Your Enlightened Lordship, it was due to my wholehearted devotion to the empire that you forgave my filthiness and disgrace, granting me your complete assistance that has resulted in the salary and rank I hold today. I am besotted and intoxicated from time to time, erring in my speech. It was due to your compassion and grace that you tolerated such and did not sentence me. You allowed me to maintain everything and you preserved my life. Even if I am sure to restrain myself, hold myself accountable, correct my errors, and tender my life, swearing such to the gods and spirits, if I am of no service, then I cannot show my face.”
Hence,[Zhuge] Liangsent[Liu] Yanback toChengdu[成都], his rank and position remaining as before.
[Liu] Yanwould lose his mind and act bewildered. In the first month of the twelfth year [234], the wife of[Liu] Yan,Lady Hu, entered [the palace] to congratulate theEmpress Dowager.2TheEmpress Dowagerordered thatLady Huremain. After several months, she left.Lady Huhad a beautiful appearance, so[Liu] Yansuspected that she and theLatter Lordhad something intimate, so he had five hundred soldiers beat[Lady] Husuch that they used their shoes to strike her face. Later, she was cast out by herself.[Lady] Hureported[Liu] Yan, and[Liu] Yanwas implicated and imprisoned. Thedirecting officialsstated, “A soldier may not beat the wife of a man and a face may not be the treading ground of a shoe.”[Liu] Yanwas eventually exposed in the market [after being executed]. From this, the wives and mothers of dignitaries ceased to celebrate incourt.•••
The celebration was likely done because of the new year.↩
